title: Public sentiment shift following Facebook's corporate rebrand to Meta and the strategic pivot toward the Metaverse

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Baseline sentiment prior to rebrand announcement
Estimated: Increased skepticism following Facebook Papers leaks

events.description: Media reports began circulating regarding Facebook's internal plans to rebrand the company and change its corporate name.
CEO Mark Zuckerberg addressed public criticism of the company's social services during the Q3 2021 earnings call while emphasizing a strategic pivot toward the metaverse.
Mark Zuckerberg officially announced at the Facebook Connect conference that the parent company would be renamed Meta Platforms, Inc.
The company formally outlined its 'metaverse-first' strategy, aiming to build an interconnected digital ecosystem using VR and AR technologies.

agents.name: Mark Zuckerberg
agents.description: CEO of Meta, primary driver of the metaverse pivot.

agents.name: Frances Haugen
agents.description: Whistleblower whose testimony significantly damaged public trust.

agents.name: Tech Journalists
agents.description: Media outlets covering the rebrand as a distraction from regulatory scrutiny.

agents.name: Early Adopters
agents.description: Tech enthusiasts interested in VR/AR and virtual worlds.

agents.name: General Public
agents.description: Average social media users skeptical of Facebook's privacy record.

agents.name: US Congress
agents.description: Legislators focused on antitrust and data privacy regulation.

notes: Pro-side is defined as positive sentiment toward Meta's pivot to the metaverse and the corporate rebrand. Against-side is defined as negative sentiment, often linked to skepticism regarding Facebook's privacy practices and the perceived distraction of the rebrand. Data quality note: Early rounds are estimated based on pre-rebrand sentiment trends; Round 4 is anchored to actual polling data.
